palette_hint: Background #F4F6FA (pale blue-grey), body text #1A1F2E (near-black navy), accent primary #2563EB (cobalt blue), accent secondary #F59E0B (amber) for highlights and badges.
type_style: Headings in system-ui bold (700) at tight letter-spacing for a technical feel; body and table cells in monospace (Courier New / ui-monospace) at 13–14px for numeric alignment; labels in sans-serif 600.
description: Nucleon Cloud Benchmark is a detailed side-by-side comparison site for five competing cloud hosting tiers — Nano, Micro, Standard, Pro, and Enterprise — covering compute specs, storage pricing, network limits, and a live cost estimator form.
constraints: All comparison tables must have sticky <thead> with background #1A1F2E and white text, zebra striping using nth-child(even) rows at #EBF0FB, and row hover highlight at #DBEAFE.
Numeric columns (price, vCPUs, RAM GB, IOPS, Mbps) must be right-aligned in ui-monospace font, minimum column width 90px, with a thin 1px right border #CBD5E1 separating each tier column.
Custom-styled checkboxes and radios on the cost-calculator page: 16×16px boxes with 2px solid #2563EB border, cobalt fill on :checked, no default browser appearance via appearance:none.
All <input>, <select> elements share a unified style: border 1px solid #94A3B8, border-radius 4px, padding 6px 10px, focus outline 2px solid #2563EB with no default outline, background #FFFFFF.
Max content width 1200px centered with 24px horizontal page padding; table wrapper allows horizontal scroll on viewports below 900px with a visible scrollbar.

page_specs.overview: A full-width introductory comparison table listing all five tiers (Nano through Enterprise) across 12 key attributes: monthly base price, vCPUs, RAM, SSD storage, bandwidth, uptime SLA, support tier, backup frequency, dedicated IP, auto-scaling, managed DB, and free trial days. Each attribute row has a left-fixed label column (180px wide, background #F4F6FA, font-weight 600) and five tier columns. Boolean features use CSS-only checkmark (✓ in #2563EB) and cross (✗ in #EF4444) characters. A sticky header row shows tier names with an amber badge on the 'Pro' column reading 'Most Popular'.
page_specs.compute-plans: A dense data table comparing CPU and memory configurations for each tier: vCPU count (1/2/4/8/16), base clock GHz, burst clock GHz, RAM GB (1/2/8/16/64), RAM type (DDR4/DDR5), L3 cache MB, hypervisor (KVM/Xen), dedicated vs shared cores, and hourly compute cost in USD. All numeric values are right-aligned monospace. Below the table, a secondary table compares GPU add-on options (None / T4 / A10G / A100) with VRAM GB, CUDA cores, and per-hour surcharge, with zebra striping. A filter row of five radio buttons (one per tier) styled with custom CSS allows visual highlighting of a selected column via a CSS :checked ~ sibling trick.
page_specs.storage-addons: A comparison table for storage products across four categories — Block SSD, Object Storage, Cold Archive, and NFS Share — each with columns for min size GB, max size TB, IOPS baseline, IOPS burst, throughput MB/s, replication factor, snapshot support, and price per GB/month. Tier eligibility is shown with filled/empty circles. Below the table, an add-on configuration form uses custom-styled checkboxes to select storage types, a <select> for replication zone (us-east-1, eu-west-2, ap-southeast-1), and a numeric <input> for desired GB, all laid out in a two-column grid with 16px gutter.
page_specs.network-features: A feature matrix table with 20 rows covering network capabilities: IPv4/IPv6 dual-stack, private VLAN, DDoS protection tier (Basic/Advanced/Enterprise), CDN edge nodes count, global load balancer, anycast DNS, BGP sessions, port speed Mbps (100/200/1000/10000/25000), monthly egress TB included, overage per GB USD, firewall rules limit, VPN gateway, private peering, latency SLA ms, and geo-restriction rules. Tier columns are color-coded in the header: Nano #94A3B8, Micro #64748B, Standard #2563EB, Pro #F59E0B, Enterprise #1A1F2E. Rows with numeric data are right-aligned monospace; rows with text values are left-aligned.
page_specs.cost-calculator: A multi-column form (two-column layout at 560px+, single column below) that lets users configure a hypothetical stack and see a price breakdown table below. Form fields include: region <select>, compute tier radio group (5 options with custom styled radios), quantity <input type='number'>, storage type checkboxes (Block/Object/Archive), storage GB <input>, bandwidth TB <input>, support tier radio (Community/Business/Enterprise), and backup frequency <select>. Input validation states use :required:invalid with a 1px solid #EF4444 border and :valid with #22C55E. Submitting is not functional; a static results table below shows per-line-item cost rows (Compute, Storage, Bandwidth, Support, Backup) with right-aligned monospace USD values, a subtotal row in bold, and an estimated annual total row with amber background #FEF3C7.
